codeReview.summary: The new screen matches the intended CTA and retry flow, but the shipped lockout handling does not line up with the real error surface coming from the biometric vault, so a real lockout can miss the distinct inline lockout state required by the feature.

issues.description: `LOCKOUT_CODES` only recognizes the raw native codes `BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT` and `BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT_PERMANENT`, but `BiometricVault.mapNativeErrorToVaultError()` normalizes both of those into `VAULT_ERROR` before `initializeFirstLaunch()` rethrows them (src/lib/enbox/biometric-vault.ts:433-437). In the real store-backed flow, setup lockouts therefore fall through to the generic branch at lines 111-116 instead of the dedicated lockout branch at lines 109-110, so the screen does not reliably show the required clear lockout message. The added tests only mock the raw codes (src/features/auth/screens/biometric-setup-screen.test.tsx:196-245), so this mismatch is not covered.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`, but the transcript skeleton does not show the mandatory context reads required by `mobile-feature-worker` before making that claim.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md` lines 12-28 require reading `mission.md` plus relevant library docs (`architecture.md`, `environment.md`, and `user-testing.md` for anchor-heavy UI work). The `worker-transcripts.jsonl` entry for session `db125975-5aee-4026-8034-4dad285ebb32` shows reads for `AGENTS.md`, `validation-contract.md`, and local source files, but no reads of `mission.md`, `.factory/library/architecture.md`, `.factory/library/environment.md`, or `.factory/library/user-testing.md`, while the handoff still reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Review failed: the feature's raw-code tests pass, but the committed screen misses the real mapped lockout error path from `initializeFirstLaunch()`, so lockout handling is not correct in production.
